description_long: This object is a vintage folding bellows camera, featuring a black leatherette-covered metal body with a prominent accordion-style bellows extending from the main housing to the lens assembly. The front of the camera displays a circular lens surrounded by a metal ring with adjustment dials and a shutter release lever. The sides reveal metal struts that support the extended bellows and a small viewfinder window integrated into the body. The top of the camera has a winding knob and a folding mechanism for compact storage. The back is mostly flat and covered in textured material, with a film advance knob and a latch for opening the camera. The overall design is utilitarian and robust, typical of early to mid-20th-century portable cameras.
description: A vintage folding bellows camera with a black leatherette body, metal struts, and a circular lens assembly. The design features an extendable bellows and compact, robust housing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular lens, adjustment dials, and the extended bellows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the accordion bellows, metal support struts, and the viewfinder window.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat, textured back, film advance knob, and latch mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the bellows, metal struts, and the winding knob on top.
